title: Here’s the latest.
description: The police said that the threat had been “neutralized,” but did not confirm the report that the attack had been fatal.
author: Tim Arango, Neil Vigdor and Pooja Salhotra

title: What to Know About San Diego’s Islamic Center
description: A visitor said that the mosque is highly diverse. Its services are in English, because attendees come from all over the world.
author: Adeel Hassan

title: Nancy Pelosi Endorses Connie Chan to Succeed Her
description: Representative Nancy Pelosi, the first woman to serve as speaker of the House, broke her silence on Monday about the race to succeed her and endorsed Connie Chan, a San Francisco official.
author: Heather Knight

title: Why Josh Shapiro Isn’t Acting Like Other Potential 2028 Candidates
description: The Pennsylvania governor’s in-state focus is a contrast with other possible presidential hopefuls.
author: Katie Glueck

title: Mark Fuhrman, Flawed Witness in O.J. Simpson Trial, Dies at 74
description: A Los Angeles police detective, he was discredited during Mr. Simpson’s 1995 murder trial by defense lawyers who pointed to his past use of racist language.
author: Clyde Haberman

title: Harvard Asks Federal Judge to Dismiss Trump Lawsuit
description: The Trump administration sued Harvard earlier this year over allegations it had not done enough to combat antisemitism on campus.
author: Mark Arsenault

title: Channel Islands Fire Started Accidentally by Shipwrecked Sailor
description: The shipwrecked man was rescued from Channel Islands National Park, but the flares set off a 10,000-acre fire that has burned park buildings, forced evacuations and threatened rare Torrey pines.
author: Soumya Karlamangla

title: American Man Tests Positive for Ebola Amid Outbreak, Officials Say
description: An American doctor working with a Christian missionary organization in the Democratic Republic of Congo was exposed to the deadly virus while treating patients, the organization said.
author: Yan Zhuang and Hannah Ziegler

title: Trump Says He Authorized New Strikes on Iran, but Has Decided to Hold Off
description: President Trump has repeatedly threatened new military action against Iran, only to pull back from resuming major fighting in a deeply unpopular, expensive conflict.
author: Tyler Pager and Eric Schmitt

title: Citing Ebola Outbreak, U.S. Restricts Entry From Congo, Uganda and South Sudan
description: The order is in effect for 30 days and does not apply to American citizens or U.S. service members.
author: Zolan Kanno-Youngs and Maggie Haberman

title: Guard Killed in Shooting at San Diego Islamic Center, Mosque Leader Says
description: The police said the situation at the county’s largest mosque remained “active,” but did not confirm the report that at least one person had been killed.
author: Tim Arango and Neil Vigdor

title: Sandy Fire in Simi Valley Forces Immediate Evacuations
description: Officials ordered more than 23,000 to evacuate after a brush fire broke out in Simi Valley, Calif.
author: Soumya Karlamangla and Amy Graff

title: American Hantavirus Patient Ordered to Stay in Quarantine Despite Plans to Leave
description: A woman from the cruise ship with an outbreak of the deadly disease has been ordered to remain in an Omaha facility for two more weeks. She says she tested negative.
author: Sonia A. Rao

title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
description: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.
author: Ernesto Londoño and Mitch Smith

title: L.A. County Response to Altadena Fire Was Not Biased, Report Finds
description: An examination of the disaster commissioned by the county concluded that its efforts were muddled and chaotic, but said that it had not discriminated against Black residents.
author: Jill Cowan

title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
description: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.
author: Andrew Duehren

title: Georgia Republicans Grasp for a Contender to Take On Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff
description: On the eve of their primary, Republicans have grown nervous about their prospects in November against Mr. Ossoff, regarded as the most vulnerable Democratic senator in the fall midterms.
author: Tim Balk

title: Justice Dept. Creates $1.8 Billion Fund to Compensate ‘Victims of Lawfare’
description: The arrangement was denounced by critics as a slush fund for supporters of President Trump, possibly including Jan. 6 rioters.
author: Glenn Thrush, Andrew Duehren and Alan Feuer

title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
description: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.
author: Pam Belluck and Sheryl Gay Stolberg

title: Northwestern Names Mung Chiang as President After Tumult Over Protests
description: The previous president resigned after months of conflict over how the university handled protests. The new president, Mung Chiang, currently leads Purdue.
author: Stephanie Saul
